commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the gateway approval-runtime token helper to prefer an existing `exec-approvals.json` socket token while keeping a process-local fallback and adding focused tests.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +105. Total +127 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main only exposes a process-local approval runtime token, so a separate process sharing only `exec-approvals.json` cannot know it. I did not rerun the live smoke, and current-head after-fix proof needs refresh.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is indicated; the remaining work is current-head proof refresh plus maintainer review of the approval-runtime auth boundary.

target.title: fix(gateway): share approval runtime socket token
target.body:
## Summary
- Use the `exec-approvals.json` socket token as the approval-runtime token when it exists, so Docker/CLI/separate-process approval clients sharing `OPENCLAW_HOME` can authenticate as the approval runtime.
- Keep the existing process-local random token as a fallback when no shared socket token exists, without creating `exec-approvals.json` just to read a token.
- Keep `approvalRuntimeToken` attached only for default or loopback-resolved gateway URLs; explicit remote gateway URLs still omit it.

## Scope split
- Split out from #86771.
- This PR is only shared approval-runtime token selection for Docker/CLI/separate-process approval clients sharing OpenClaw state.
- It does not include stale Discord button UX or the `approval.routeNotice.send` gateway method.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: A gateway approval-runtime client can use a token loaded from the shared `exec-approvals.json` socket record, allowing a local/loopback approval client that shares OpenClaw state to resolve a requester-bound approval. Explicit remote gateway URLs remain outside the token-sharing path.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on this PR head `cff56e37ff`, Node `v24.14.0`, macOS host. The smoke used an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`, a real temp `exec-approvals.json`, a real loopback gateway server, and real gateway clients.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a `pnpm exec tsx` smoke from the repo root that wrote a temp `.openclaw/exec-approvals.json` with `socket.token=shared-runtime-smoke-token`, verified `getOperatorApprovalRuntimeToken()` selected it, started a real loopback gateway, created a pending exec approval through one gateway client, and resolved it through `withOperatorApprovalsGatewayClient({ gatewayUrl: loopbackUrl })`.

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm exec tsx <shared-runtime-token-smoke>
[gateway] starting HTTP server...
[gateway] http server listening (0 plugins)
[gateway] ready
SMOKE shared_socket_runtime_token ok {"selectedToken":"shared-runtime-smoke-token","gatewayUrl":"ws://127.0.0.1:<port>","approvalId":"shared-runtime-smoke-approval","decision":"allow-once"}
[shutdown] started: gateway stopping
[shutdown] completed cleanly in 12ms
```

Observed result after fix: The approval-runtime token came from the shared socket token file, the loopback explicit gateway client could read and resolve the pending requester-bound approval, and the original requester observed `allow-once`.

What was not tested: No Docker Compose two-process smoke was run. The live smoke used one Node process with a real temp shared state file and real loopback gateway clients; focused tests cover fallback-token stability, late shared-token appearance, and remote explicit `gatewayUrl` token omission.

## Tests and validation
- `PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-approval-runtime-token.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts --reporter=dot` — 5 files, 23 tests passed.
- `PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/operator-approval-runtime-token.ts src/gateway/operator-approval-runtime-token.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` reported one finding about sending `approvalRuntimeToken` to explicit loopback overrides. I rejected that finding for this PR because the maintainer-requested contract is to keep the token for resolved local/loopback gateway URLs while continuing to omit it for explicit remote gateway URLs.

## Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`

Highest-risk area: approval-runtime authentication for local approval clients sharing OpenClaw state.

Mitigation: shared-token selection only reads an existing socket token; it does not create the shared file. The approval client attaches the runtime token only for default or loopback-resolved gateway URLs, while explicit remote gateway URLs omit it.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
